steps.text: Convert square feet to square meters: since \(1 \text{ m} = 3.28 \text{ ft}\), we have \(1 \text{ m}^{2} = (3.28)^{2} \text{ ft}^{2} = 10.7584 \text{ ft}^{2}\).
Convert hours to minutes: \(1 \text{ hr} = 60 \text{ min}\).

Apply both conversions: \(270 \frac{\text{ft}^{2}}{\text{hr}} \times \frac{1 \text{ m}^{2}}{10.7584 \text{ ft}^{2}} \times \frac{1 \text{ hr}}{60 \text{ min}} = \frac{270}{10.7584 \times 60} \approx \frac{270}{645.5} \approx 0.418 \frac{\text{m}^{2}}{\text{min}}\). Evaluate each choice.

choices.explanation: • 0.42 is the closest value to 0.418. That matches choice A — A is correct.
• 1.37 would result from dividing 270 by 3.28 and then by 60, applying only a linear (not squared) conversion for area. Not correct.
• 14.76 would result from dividing 270 by 10.7584 but forgetting to convert hours to minutes. Not correct.
• 25.10 would result from dividing 270 by 10.7584 with no per-minute conversion (rate stays per hour). Not correct.
